title: The Threat of Catastrophic Forgetting
concept: If trained too aggressively on domain data, the model can 'forget' how to write general, grammatically correct sentences, requiring a balanced training mix.

exercises.prompt: What can happen if we train an AI model only on specialized data without any general language examples?

options.text: It might forget how to write normal, grammatically correct sentences.
It will automatically become smarter at all tasks.
It will run faster and use less electricity.

exercises.explain: When an AI is trained too aggressively on new, specialized information, it can overwrite and forget its general language foundations.

cards.title: The Memory Wipe Danger
cards.body: Imagine training an AI to be a super-smart doctor, only for it to completely forget how to say hello! When AIs learn new things too fast, they can suffer from sudden, catastrophic memory loss.

cards.title: What is Catastrophic Forgetting?
cards.body: Catastrophic forgetting happens when an AI is trained so aggressively on new, specific data that it overrides and forgets the general language rules and vocabulary it previously learned.

cards.title: The Tennis Practice Pitfall
cards.body: Imagine you are learning to play tennis. If you spend three weeks doing nothing but practicing a backhand swing, you might completely forget how to serve or run! An AI does the same: focus too much on one skill, and the old ones start to slip away.

cards.title: The Medical Bot Meltdown
cards.body: A customer service AI is trained heavily on medical codes. Suddenly, it starts outputting gibberish when asked to say 'Hello, how can I help you today?'. It learned the medical jargon but forgot basic human politeness!

cards.title: Myth: AIs Never Forget
cards.body: AIs don't have infinite, permanent memory banks like a hard drive. When they learn new patterns, the old patterns are literally overwritten unless we actively work to protect them.

cards.title: Training Diets Compared
cards.body: See how the training diet affects what the AI can do. Focusing only on one food group ruins the balance!

left.text: Trains on both specific medical texts and general web conversations. Result: Smart doctor that speaks clearly.

right.text: Trains only on medical terms. Result: Knows drug names, but speaks in broken, ungrammatical sentences.

exercises.prompt: Why does an AI model lose its general grammar skills when trained too aggressively on specific domain data?

options.text: The model's database runs out of physical hard drive space.
The new domain data overwrites its general language knowledge.
The computer hosting the AI gets too hot and resets.
The AI decides it no longer wants to speak to humans.

exercises.explain: If a model is trained only on specific domain data, it will overwrite the general language structures it needs to write proper sentences.

exercises.prompt: True or False: To prevent forgetting, we should train the model on a mix of both new domain data and general language data.
exercises.explain: A balanced training mix containing both new specialized data and general language data is required to keep the model's basic skills intact.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the term used for when an AI loses its general skills after learning new specific data.
exercises.sentence: Overly aggressive training on specialized data can lead to catastrophic forgetting of basic skills.
exercises.explain: Catastrophic forgetting occurs when a model is trained too aggressively on new data and loses its previous abilities.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs shows a model suffering from catastrophic forgetting after being trained on medical data?

options.text: Response A: 'Please consult your doctor about taking ibuprofen for pain.'
Response B: 'Patient ibuprofen take mg 400 normal headache yes go.'
Response C: 'I cannot answer medical questions. Please ask a doctor.'

exercises.explain: Response B represents a model suffering from catastrophic forgetting, as it has lost the ability to structure a normal sentence while trying to use medical terms.

exercises.prompt: An AI developer wants to teach a model about legal documents without letting it forget how to write normal, everyday emails. What is the best strategy?

options.text: Mix general text with the new domain data during training.
Train the model exclusively on the new domain data.
Delete the model's original language files entirely.
Only train the model for five minutes at a time.

exercises.explain: To prevent catastrophic forgetting, developers use a balanced training mix. This blends new domain data (like medical records) with general language data, ensuring the model retains its basic grammar and conversational skills while learning the new domain.
